_meta.note: Scores manually verified by client (Lucie). Used as authoritative override in generate_pcaf_assessment.py.

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: 17/18 coefficient coverage (94.4%) – all 7 asset classes covered for S1&S2+S3 except sovereign debt S3

Attribution Methodology.evidence: Full PCAF methodology per asset class (PCAF signatory)

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: No facilitated emissions reported (Part B not yet implemented)
N/A – Nordea has no P&C insurance activity; life insurance emissions included in financed emissions
18/18 coefficient coverage (100%) – full coverage of all asset classes including sovereign debt S3 (consumption-based)

Attribution Methodology.evidence: Full PCAF methods per asset class (PCAF signatory)

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: Insurance-related emissions published within financed emissions (Scopes 1, 2 and 3) but no breakdown by insurance branch – 50% coverage estimated (4/8 coefficient)

Temporal Coverage.evidence: No dedicated historical series for insurance-associated emissions

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: 6/18 coefficient coverage (33.3%) – commercial real estate (1/1, S1&S2+S3 in 2025) + listed equity/corporate bonds (3/6, S1&S2 only, PCAF database DQS 4) + sovereign debt (0.5/2, basic S1&S2 via PPP-GDP, no LULUCF/S3) + project finance (1/2, N/A CRE specialist) + mortgages (0.25/0.5, runoff portfolio excluded) + motor vehicle (0.25/0.5, N/A); corporate loans to financial institutions (~€5.8 bn) not covered in PCAF reporting

Data Quality Score.evidence: Average PCAF DQS 3.3 as at 31 Dec 2025, weighted by financed emissions for the first time (previously unweighted); CREF portfolio DQS ranges from 2.0–4.0 by country, Treasury portfolio DQS 4 (database factors)

Attribution Methodology.evidence: PCAF-aligned methodology per covered asset class: CREF uses loan/property value attribution factor (closely based on PCAF but explicitly noted as not fully compliant); Treasury uses EVIC for corporate bonds and PPP-adjusted GDP for sovereign. Active PCAF member (D/A/CH/LI network, CRE subgroup) and published dedicated PCAF Financed Emissions Report for FY2024. Does not cover all asset classes.

Portfolio Coverage.evidence: CREF portfolio €34.0 bn + Treasury (sovereign + corporate bonds ~€8-10 bn) = ~€42-44 bn covered out of ~€47.8 bn total assets (~€44 bn investable); loans to financial institutions (~€5.8 bn) excluded; estimated coverage ~85-90%

Temporal Coverage.evidence: 3 years of CREF financed emissions (2023: 639,681 t CO2; 2024: 607,197 t CO2; 2025: 632,812 t CO2) with carbon intensity trends + 2030 target (33 kg CO2/m²) and 2050 corridor (1–6 kg CO2/m²) from formal transition plan

Asset Class Coverage.evidence: No facilitated emissions reported. Aareal is a specialized CRE lender with no primary-market underwriting or capital markets facilitation activities.
N/A – Aareal is a pure CRE bank with no insurance underwriting activities

Data Quality Score.evidence: [Verified from XHTML, L22989] Overall DQS S1&S2 = 4.0. Per asset class: Mortgages 3.9, CRE WB 3.7, CRE BB 3.3, Corporate BB 5.0, Corporate WB 4.0, Equity 4.9, Bonds 3.6. Range 3.5-4.5 -> Score 2.

Attribution Methodology.evidence: [Verified from XHTML, L22329] 'calculated in accordance with the Partnership for Carbon Accounting Financials (PCAF) methodology'. SBTi 1.5C validated (L19967). PCAF signatory not explicitly stated in report. Score 4.
